name: Willie Bryant
abstract: William Stevens Bryant (August 30, 1908 – February 9, 1964) was an American jazz bandleader, vocalist, and disc jockey, known as the "Mayor of Harlem".

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Pittsburgh Courier Poll Winners Jazz Concert
Midnight Performance: Pittsburgh Courier Poll Winners Jazz Concert
Midnight Performance: Pittsburgh Courier Charity Fund's 7th Annual Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I remember I wrote that "Cavernism" arrangement for Willie Bryant's band, after I joined Willie Bryant

about_quotes.speaker: David Albert Francis
about_quotes.evidence: We played the Savoy, so I was up there for six more months with Willie Bryant.

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: With Willie Bryant, yeah. ... Yeah, when I was with Willie Bryant's Orchestra.

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: I worked opposite Chick's band up there while I was with Willie Bryant.

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Green
about_quotes.evidence: Well, it was originated by Willie Bryant, partner of Leonard Reed and Ralph Cooper, but he was an orchestra leader so he gave it to the choreographer, Teddy Blackman, to teach to the chorus girls as an education dance.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer mentions Willie Bryant was presented at the same time the interviewee was in New York, but the interviewee only says 'Yeah' without confirming a personal connection.
